envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Team-tier proposed actions materialize from W3 Stage 2 workflow recommendations per ADR-0081 §2.2. Every proposed action stays at proposed_action_state: PROPOSED_NOT_AUTHORIZED. Runtime promotion to Section 2 Action requires separate Founder authorization per slice + ADR-0026 dual-control where required.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Team envelope materializes 4 Stage 3 proposed actions from the 6 Team W3 recommendations. Sprint risk + travel expense + onboarding + offboarding surface here; the daily-brief + weekly-execution Stage 2 recommendations stay at Stage 2 at this tier (no per-action surface needed at Team — the recommendation itself is the unit of value at this tier).
audit_expectations: ADMIN_ACTION:PROPOSED_ACTION_REFERENCED (forward-substrate audit literal at runtime promotion per ADR-0042 §Q-γ.1 clean-transition) when proposed action substrate is consumed by a Twin

items.name: Sprint Risk Summary — Proposed Action (Stage 3)
items.human_readable_summary: PM Twin proposes posting a sprint risk summary to the team's Slack channel. Operator confirms before promotion; promotion to Section 2 Action requires separate Founder authorization per slice.
items.model_usage_notes: Materializes from W3 sprint-risk-summary.team.v1 recommendation. PM Twin drafts a team-scoped sprint risk summary using Jira metadata (state aggregates only per C4-A privacy) + Linear metadata (state-type aggregates per C4-B). Proposes Slack POST via the canonical SLACK_READ binding's write-capability extension at ≥C6 (forward-substrate); at Stage 3 the proposed_payload_shape is fully described but the connector write is gated.
items.safe_fallback: PM reviews the sprint risk summary independently without Twin-mediated Slack post; W3 Stage 2 recommendation surface remains available.
DMW_scope_implications: Reads aggregate Jira + Linear metadata scoped to the team DMW
Never reads per-engineer commit history, PR review timings, or individual performance data
Never surfaces raw issue titles or engineer identifiers outside the team DMW scope
audit_expectations: PROPOSED_ACTION_REFERENCED (forward-substrate) at this proposed action's read
ACTION_PROPOSED + ACTION_APPROVED + ACTION_SUCCEEDED at promotion to Section 2 Action runtime (forward-substrate; gated)
safe_defaults: Proposal stays at PROPOSED_NOT_AUTHORIZED until operator review

items.name: Travel Booking + Expense Shell — Proposed Action (Stage 3)
items.human_readable_summary: EA Twin proposes a draft travel itinerary + expense shell for an executive trip. Operator confirms before any external booking system is touched.
items.model_usage_notes: Materializes from W3 travel-booking-expense-shell.team.v1 recommendation per Wave 2.1 §22 EA aha moment. At Stage 3 the EA Twin produces a draft itinerary + expense shell as an internal-only proposed action; the actual external booking system invocation (TravelEdge / Concur / Navan / etc.) is forward-substrate at a later C-slice that requires its own Founder authorization. This proposed action stays INTERNAL_ONLY at Team tier.
items.safe_fallback: EA composes travel + expense draft independently; the W3 Stage 2 recommendation remains available as a structural template.
DMW_scope_implications: Reads executive's calendar metadata only (no per-attendee email PII)
Reads executive's stated travel preferences from the executive DMW scope
Never reads personal financial data; expense shell is a template, not a financial transaction
audit_expectations: PROPOSED_ACTION_REFERENCED (forward-substrate) at this proposed action's read
safe_defaults: Internal-only at Team tier (no external booking system call)
Dual-control mandatory per ADR-0026 (EA + delegated authority holder)
Aggregate-only retention class (no per-trip details retained at substrate tier)
forbidden_defaults: External booking system invocation at Team tier

items.name: Onboarding Checklist — Proposed Action (Stage 3)
items.human_readable_summary: Admin Twin proposes a specific onboarding checklist instance for a new team member. Operator confirms before any internal-record mutation.
items.model_usage_notes: Materializes from W3 onboarding-checklist.team.v1 recommendation. Admin Twin proposes a per-team-member onboarding checklist instance. INTERNAL_ONLY at Team tier — actual provisioning into external systems (Slack workspace invite / Linear seat / GitHub repo access / Google Workspace seat / Microsoft 365 seat / etc.) is forward-substrate per ADR-0084 ≥C6.
items.safe_fallback: Admin walks the OOTB onboarding template manually; W3 Stage 2 recommendation surface remains available.
DMW_scope_implications: Reads team membership metadata only (no per-employee performance data)
Operates within the admin scope; never expands to manager-surveillance scope
Onboarded user's personal-tier DMW is not provisioned by this proposed action; provisioning is forward-substrate at runtime promotion
audit_expectations: PROPOSED_ACTION_REFERENCED (forward-substrate) at this proposed action's read

items.name: Offboarding Checklist — Proposed Action (Stage 3)
items.human_readable_summary: Admin Twin proposes an offboarding checklist instance for a departing team member. Audit chain integrity per RULE 10 + ADR-0002 preserved.
items.model_usage_notes: Materializes from W3 offboarding-checklist.team.v1 recommendation. Admin Twin proposes the per-departing-team-member offboarding checklist. INTERNAL_ONLY at Team tier — actual deprovisioning from external systems is forward-substrate. Per RULE 10 the departing user's audit history is never deleted; deleted_at timestamps are set per ADR-0002 BEFORE DELETE trigger.
items.safe_fallback: Admin walks the OOTB offboarding template manually; audit chain integrity preserved per ADR-0002.
DMW_scope_implications: Reads team membership metadata only (no per-employee performance / compensation / termination-reason data)
Departing user's personal-tier DMW is preserved per RULE 10 (deleted_at set; rows preserved)
Cross-team audit history preservation per ADR-0002 BEFORE DELETE trigger
audit_expectations: PROPOSED_ACTION_REFERENCED (forward-substrate) at this proposed action's read
